title: German text during Asuka mindrape
wikitext:
[]: German text during Asuka mindrape             Quote Post by nomis1242 » Mon Dec 27, 2010 9:02 am Hi, I've made an attempt to translate the German text which quickly flashes on the screen during Asuka's Ep 22 mindrape. Can anyone else who speaks German have a look through and let me know what they think please? If we can get some consensus on the translation, it'd be great to put this on the wiki I reckon. (Asuka clutching at head- start of attack) Nein - No Erhǟngte - Hanged herself * (...Unit 02 convulsing...) Nein - No Groll - Resentment (or grudge) (Unit 02 falling over) Nein Menarche - http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Menarche (Asuka clutching head) Nein Schmach - Shame (Unit 02 hands on knees) Nein Doppelselbstmord begehen - Double suicide (hard to read, so this is debatable, but “double suicide" seems likely given the context) (close-up of Unit 02 head) Nein Ausdauer / Ansehen - endurance / observe (eg to look at something)* (Unit 02 eyes flashing) Nein Wie ǟrgerlich - How angering (“annoying” is direct translation, but this is too mild) (flashback- Asuka stamps on doll) Peinliche - embarrassing (or there's a slight chance they meant "painful") Steiffmutter - stepmother Detverlill (??? can’t read properly) / der Verlust- ??? / the loss** Peinliche - embarrassing (or there's a slight chance they meant "painful") Wahnsinnig - insane (door opening to sky) Nein / Japanese / Tod - No / ??? / Death * thanks to Kendrix for these translations ** thanks to Literal Translation Project for these translations

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Mon Dec 27, 2010 9:18 am http://forum.evageeks.org/viewtopic.php?p=260660#260660

[]:             Quote Post by Kendrix » Mon Dec 27, 2010 10:14 am Mr. Tines wrote: http://forum.evageeks.org/viewtopic.php?p=260660#260660

[]:             Quote Post by nomis1242 » Mon Dec 27, 2010 5:43 pm Thanks Mr Tines and Kendrix. I've fixed up my OP in case anyone stumbles across it.

[]:             Quote Post by Kendrix » Mon Dec 27, 2010 8:12 pm nomis1242 wrote: Thanks Mr Tines and Kendrix. I've fixed up my OP in case anyone stumbles across it.

[]:             Quote Post by nomis1242 » Tue Dec 28, 2010 12:22 am ^ Thanks again. More fixing of OP. Kendrix wrote: But "peinlich" is embarassing in any context ... I guess someone translated it as 'painfull' because of the similarity to the english word - taking that road, you're usually right in 99% of the cases

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Tue Dec 28, 2010 4:09 am nomis1242 wrote: Google Translate

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Tue Dec 28, 2010 12:30 pm Mr. Tines wrote: In 1995?

[]:             Quote Post by Kendrix » Tue Dec 28, 2010 1:22 pm they used GOOGLE TRANSLATE? ...That explains A LOT. At least they got most of the English right.

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Tue Dec 28, 2010 1:43 pm Kendrix wrote: they used GOOGLE TRANSLATE?

[]:             Quote Post by nomis1242 » Tue Dec 28, 2010 4:29 pm Bagheera wrote: No. They were not time travelers, so they had to do things the old fashioned way.

[]:             Quote Post by Kendrix » Tue Dec 28, 2010 4:37 pm They might as well have used it, tough...

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Tue Dec 28, 2010 4:58 pm Kendrix wrote: They might as well have used it, tough...

[]:             Quote Post by Kendrix » Tue Dec 28, 2010 10:15 pm Bagheera wrote: Cut 'em some slack. It's not like we're any better at rendering things in kanji.

[]:             Quote Post by Azathoth » Tue Dec 28, 2010 10:37 pm Kendrix wrote: And I have to admit that German has a grammar as difficult as Latin.

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Tue Dec 28, 2010 10:42 pm Kendrix wrote: And I have to admit that German has a grammar as difficult as Latin.

[]:             Quote Post by TehDonutKing » Wed Dec 29, 2010 12:18 am And English is the second ugliest language I've ever heard. The first is Klingon.

[]:             Quote Post by die-yng » Wed Dec 29, 2010 2:03 am It's funny, I as a german would agree with Kendrix (also german) about the grammar being too complicated, but non-germans see it different, so perhaps it is easier for non-native speakers. On the other hand I found english to be a real simple and easy to learn language, compared to, let's say french. f.e. As far as some of the german words being out of date, wrong or rather strange choices, believe me, there is much worse. I'm just saying, american comics and movies. Most of the time the german there is totally absurd.

[]:             Quote Post by Kendrix » Wed Dec 29, 2010 12:57 pm die-yng wrote: On the other hand I found english to be a real simple and easy to learn language, compared to, let's say french. f.e. .

[]:             Quote Post by Galaxy News Reidio » Wed Dec 29, 2010 1:02 pm On that "Angering/Annoying", might "Enraging" or "Infuriating" be a better word?
